project: TypeScript 6.0 RC CI Support and Compatibility
description: TypeScript 6.0 has a release candidate available. The Inngest JS SDK already tests against multiple TypeScript versions in CI (latest, ~5.9.0, ~5.8.0) via a matrix strategy in the PR workflow. This effort adds TS 6.0 RC to that matrix—covering test:types, test:dist, and test:composite—and fixes any type errors or incompatibilities surfaced by the new compiler version. The peer dependency range (>=5.8.0) will not change until TS 6.0 reaches stable.

userStories.title: Add TS 6.0 RC to the CI type-test matrix
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want the PR workflow to test the SDK against TypeScript 6.0 RC so that I'm alerted to incompatibilities before merging.
acceptanceCriteria: .github/workflows/pr.yml inngest_types job matrix includes a "rc" entry (installs typescript@rc)
The rc matrix entry runs pnpm run test:types (same as other entries)
The rc matrix entry runs pnpm run test:dist (same as other entries)
The job is required to pass (no continue-on-error) — same enforcement as other TS versions
Existing matrix entries (latest, ~5.9.0, ~5.8.0) are unchanged
fail-fast: false is preserved so all versions report independently

userStories.title: Add TS 6.0 RC composite test to CI
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want a CI job that runs the composite (end-to-end packaged) type test against TS 6.0 RC so that consumers of the published package are validated against the new compiler.
acceptanceCriteria: A new CI job (or extension of the existing inngest_test_composite job) runs test:composite with TypeScript 6.0 RC
The composite test project (packages/inngest/test/composite_project) is configured to use TS 6.0 RC for this job (e.g. by overriding typescript before running npm run test)
The job must pass to merge (not informational)
The existing inngest_test_composite job (which uses the default/pinned TS version) continues to run unchanged

userStories.title: Fix type errors surfaced by TS 6.0 RC in test:types
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want all type errors reported by tsc under TS 6.0 RC to be resolved so that the SDK is forward-compatible.
acceptanceCriteria: pnpm run test:types passes when typescript@rc (6.0 RC) is installed
Fixes do not break compilation under TS 5.8, 5.9, or latest (all existing matrix entries still pass)
Fixes are minimal and do not introduce unnecessary refactoring
Any TS 6.0-specific workarounds are documented with inline comments explaining why

userStories.title: Fix type errors surfaced by TS 6.0 RC in test:dist
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want the compiled .d.ts declaration files to be valid under TS 6.0 RC so that consumers see no type errors from our package.
acceptanceCriteria: pnpm run test:dist passes when typescript@rc (6.0 RC) is installed
Fixes do not break test:dist under existing TS versions
If the issue is in the build output (tsdown/tsc config), the build config change is documented

userStories.title: Fix type errors surfaced by TS 6.0 RC in test:composite
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want the composite project test to pass under TS 6.0 RC so that real-world consumers of the packaged SDK are validated.
acceptanceCriteria: pnpm run test:composite passes when the composite project uses TS 6.0 RC
Fixes do not break test:composite under its default TS version (^5.3.3)
If composite project tsconfig.json needs changes for TS 6.0 compatibility, those changes are backward-compatible
